inbox/y24mlb57rm_PAGES352.pdf.description: The document is a PhD thesis submitted by Nicole McGill to Charles Sturt University in February 2020. It focuses on the issue of waiting lists for speech and language services for children and families. The thesis is structured into four parts: General Introduction, Desperation and Aspirations, Reimagining Services, and Conclusions and Contributions. It includes various chapters discussing stakeholders' perspectives, SLPs' views on waiting lists, the design and evaluation of a waiting list management strategy, and the implications and contributions of the research. The document also contains acknowledgements, ethical approvals, and a certificate of authorship.

inbox/patreon_connect_web_files.zip.description: The document is a compressed .zip file named 'ozn34gcvxe.zip'. It contains a directory structure with files related to a web application or website, specifically for a 'patreon-connect' project. The directory includes CSS files ('admin.css' and 'app.css') for styling, and a 'fonts' directory with a license file ('Apache License.txt') and a font file ('LibreFranklin-ExtraBold.ttf'). The purpose of this document appears to be to package and distribute the necessary files for a web application or website, likely related to connecting with Patreon services.

inbox/qak250zqau_PAGES9.pdf.description: The document is a scientific article discussing the use of pyrosequencing for quantitative methylation analysis of the SNRPN gene as a diagnostic test for Prader-Willi Syndrome (PWS) and Angelman Syndrome (AS). It details the background of these syndromes, the methodology used for the analysis, including sodium bisulfite treatment of DNA, PCR amplification, and pyrosequencing. The study compares the results of pyrosequencing with methylation-specific PCR (MS-PCR) and finds them to be 100% concordant. The article concludes that pyrosequencing is a rapid and robust method for diagnosing PWS and AS.

inbox/cognixion_one_clinical_plan.docx.description: The document titled 'Cognixion ONE Guidance Documents: Clinical Trials, Human Factors, and User Testing' by Luke Steuber, last updated on 8/25/2022, version 1.6, discusses the clinical plan for the Cognixion ONE headset. It focuses on achieving FDA 510k and PDAC approval as a Class II EEG measurement system and CMS accreditation as a Speech Generating Device (SGD) for AAC use. The document outlines the initial clinical assessments, future partnerships, and potential expansions into new software and hardware applications. It also covers human factors and user testing, including background research, testing schedules, inclusion and exclusion criteria for participants, and areas of particular diligence such as photosensitive epilepsy and allergies. The document provides a detailed summary of addressable populations for the device, categorizing them based on various criteria.

inbox/datafinch_seminar_workbook_functio.description: The document is a workbook for seminars at DataFinch, presented by Gregory P. Hanley, Ph.D., BCBA-D, focusing on the functional assessment approach. It includes an open-ended functional assessment interview template with sections for relevant background information, questions to inform the design of a functional analysis, and tactics to overcome general obstacles to conducting functional analyses. The document also lists several references related to functional analysis and problem behavior.

inbox/speakprose_ar_bci_prd.docx.description: The document is a Product Requirements Document (PRD) for the software related to Speakprose AR/BCI, a wearable headset with a speech-generating device driven by augmented reality. It outlines high-level requirements for the software, including business objectives, product overview, and specific requirements for the end user application (Speakprose AR), caregiver application (Companion App), and supporting infrastructure (Platform). The document also discusses regulatory requirements for selling the device as a medical product in the US and Canada, and includes sections on non-requirements, product risks, and document control.

inbox/5zbu4u8fly_PAGES1.pdf.description: The document is a comprehensive guide for Speech Language Pathologists (SLPs) on how to evaluate and report on a client's need for a speech-generating device (SGD). It outlines the necessary components of a speech report, including client background, evaluation date, speech and language abilities, medical diagnosis, cognitive and physical abilities, mobility, hearing and vision status, daily communication needs, rationale for device selection, SGDs considered or trialed, recommended SGD and accessories, functional communication goals, treatment plan, and a non-conflict statement with signature. The document also provides additional resources for report writing and funding.

inbox/marketing_requirements_project_yod.description: The document is a comprehensive marketing and customer requirements document for Project Yoda, identified by IMP-002. It outlines the product strategy and requirements for a product aimed at ALS patients, focusing on enhancing communication capabilities as the disease progresses. The document includes sections on market analysis, stakeholder analysis, competitor analysis, SWOT analysis, product overview, commercialization plan, and detailed product requirements. It emphasizes confidentiality and provides guidelines for document completion and formatting.

inbox/6g68jzpp9y_PAGES9.pdf.description: The document is a review article published on October 31, 2013, in the journal Frontiers in Integrative Neuroscience. It discusses the relationship between executive dysfunction and timing deficits in Parkinson's disease (PD). The article explores how cognitive symptoms in PD, such as deficits in working memory, planning, and visuospatial attention, contribute to timing deficits. It also examines the role of dopamine signaling in the striatum and the frontal cortex in timing, suggesting that timing may be considered an executive function. The article includes sections on the introduction to PD, executive dysfunction, perceptual timing, and models of timing, with references to various studies and theories.
